data.content: Your code uses `IFileSystem` directly. I'll generate a focused wrapper interface + implementation that gives you a simpler API and a single place to add cross-cutting concerns (logging, retries, etc.).
data.reasoningText:
The user has a simple .NET project that uses `System.IO.Abstractions` with `IFileSystem`. They want a file system wrapper. 

But wait - `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions` IS already the abstraction/wrapper pattern. The user is already using it. What would they mean by "generate a file system wrapper"?

I think they might be asking me to generate a wrapper class that implements `IFileSystem` or wraps `System.IO.Abstractions.FileSystem` - but that doesn't make much sense since `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides `FileSystem` as the real implementation and `MockFileSystem` for testing.
 
Looking at this again, they might want a custom facade that simplifies common operations on top of `IFileSystem`, or perhaps a concrete wrapper class that wraps the real `System.IO` calls and implements `IFileSystem` - though that's essentially what `System.IO.Abstractions.FileSystem` already does. I think the most practical approach is to create a custom `IFileSystemWrapper` interface that exposes only the methods they're actually using, then implement it as a wrapper around `IFileSystem`. This gives them a focused, testable abstraction without the overhead of the full interface. Let me trace through their code to see exactly which file operations they need.

toolCalls.name: create
create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-b96f152978224d04a803d2cfce133973/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs b/tmp/sv-b96f152978224d04a803d2cfce133973/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-b96f152978224d04a803d2cfce133973/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,86 @@
+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Default implementation of <see cref="IFileSystemWrapper"/> that
+/// delegates every call to a <see cref="IFileSystem"/> instance from
+/// System.IO.Abstractions.
+/// </summary>
+public sealed class FileSystemWrapper(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileSystemWrapper
+{
+    public IFileSystem Inner => fileSystem;
+
+    // --- File operations ---
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+
+    public byte[] ReadAllBytes(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllBytes(path);
+
+    public string[] ReadAllLines(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);
+
+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, string[] contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, contents);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream Create(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Create(path);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(sourceFileName, destFileName, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourceFileName, destFileName);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    // --- Directory operations ---
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public string[] GetFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public string[] GetDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // --- Path helpers ---
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+

+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Default implementation of <see cref="IFileSystemWrapper"/> that
+/// delegates every call to a <see cref="IFileSystem"/> instance from
+/// System.IO.Abstractions.
+/// </summary>
+public sealed class FileSystemWrapper(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileSystemWrapper
+{
+    public IFileSystem Inner => fileSystem;
+
+    // --- File operations ---
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+

+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);

+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, string[] contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, contents);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream Create(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Create(path);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(sourceFileName, destFileName, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourceFileName, destFileName);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    // --- Directory operations ---
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public string[] GetFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public string[] GetDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // --- Path helpers ---
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+

data.content:
Created two files:

- **`IFileSystemWrapper.cs`** — focused interface covering file read/write, directory ops, and path helpers. Exposes an `Inner` property as an escape hatch to the full `IFileSystem`.
- **`FileSystemWrapper.cs`** — sealed implementation that delegates every call to the injected `IFileSystem`.

**DI registration** would look like:

```csharp
services.AddSingleton<IFileSystem>(new FileSystem());
services.AddSingleton<IFileSystemWrapper, FileSystemWrapper>();
```

Your `DataService` can then depend on `IFileSystemWrapper` instead of `IFileSystem` directly, and you have a single seam for adding logging, retries, or other cross-cutting concerns.
